arts.ttl: Brendan Rodgers wants Bayern hurt in Celtic Park cauldron as footballing royalty won't get the red carpet treatment
arts.sum: The Hoops boss respects Bayern's standing in the game but has urged his men to make them suffer in Glasgow

arts.ttl: Have Celtic ever beaten Bayern Munich? How Brendan Rodgers stands on brink of history
arts.sum: So much history is on the line for Brendan Rodgers as he could become the first Celtic manager to win a knockout round leg in the Champions League era. Celtic are preparing for their first Champions League knockout game for the first time in 12 years this week.

arts.ttl: Who will win Celtic vs Bayern Munich? Our writers deliver their predictions for Champions League blockbuster
arts.sum: It's a massive night under the lights at Parkhead as the Hoops welcome a huge Euro heavyweight to Glasgow

arts.ttl: Ex-Celtic boss faces scrutiny to adapt tactics, injuries can’t justify Spurs’ woes
arts.sum: Former Celtic boss Ange Postecoglou previously insisted that he always wins silverware in the second season at a new club.

arts.ttl: Auston Trusty explains what point Celtic stars want to prove against Harry Kane and Bayern Munich in Champions League
arts.sum: He will come up against one of the world’s best strikers in the form of Harry Kane who has notched 28 goals in as many games for the Bundesliga giants this term. Trusty has been a solid defensive partner for Cameron Carter-Vickers and Liam Scales at times and he hopes to continue that against Bayern when the two clubs do battle tomorrow evening.

arts.ttl: Celtic vs. Bayern Munich: Lineups, team news, injuries, how to watch and more!
arts.sum: Bayern and Celtic have only met on four previous occasions, all in the Champions League group stage. Bayern finished the Champions League group stage in 12th place and Celtic mustered a 21st place finish.

arts.ttl: Report: Bayern Munich monitoring Croatian sensation at striker
arts.sum: According to a report from Sport Bild journalists Christian Falk and Tobi Altschäffl (as captured by @MiaSanMia ), Bayern Munich could be eyeing up a striker for the future — FC Nürnberg’s Croatian striker Tino Kusanović: At just 16, Kusanović seems far off from being considered to be any kind of successor to Harry Kane, but Bayern Munich could be keeping an eye on the future.

arts.ttl: Chelsea, Bayern Munich dealt transfer blow as Premier League defender gives 'green light' to Real Madrid switch
arts.sum: Highly-rated Bournemouth defender Dean Huijsen is reportedly keen to join Real Madrid amid interest from Chelsea and Bayern Munich.

arts.ttl: Three bold calls Brendan Rodgers could make for Celtic vs Bayern Munich Champions League tie
arts.sum: Bayern Munich will offer a stern test but with 11 goals scored in their most recent two fixtures, Rodgers is sure to be confident that his Celts can upset their Euro heavyweight opponents in front of a packed out crowd. Given the domino effect of the potential Daizen decision for Rodgers, it would mean Jota would start in the wide areas.

arts.ttl: Rodgers keen for Celtic to use home atmosphere and ‘hurt’ Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Scottish side welcome ‘football royalty’ on Wednesday Hosts boosted by Daizen Maeda return after ban appeal Brendan Rodgers wants Bayern Munich to experience “hurt” at Celtic Park after taking pride in his team’s European improvement this season. Bayern’s visit to Glasgow marks the first time since 2013 that Celtic will play knockout Champions League football, with Rodgers contemplating his most significant scalp yet. Celtic are the heavy underdogs to see off the German giants over two legs but Rodgers has challenged his players to keep the tie competitive before they head to the Allianz Arena next week. Slovan Bratislava, RB Leipzig and Young Boys have already been defeated at Celtic Park this season, with the hosts unbeaten in their last half-dozen home European games. Continue reading...
